Skill rust-docsrs-augmentation (see `.cursor/skills/rust-docsrs-augmentation/`):

1. Add `//!` at the top of `src/lib.rs` with a one-line summary, audience (CLI vs library), and a small module table.
2. Run `RUSTDOCFLAGS='-D warnings' cargo doc --no-deps -p your_crate` before publish to catch broken intra-doc links and invalid pseudo-HTML in `///` comments.
3. After `cargo publish`, https://docs.rs rebuilds API docs for the new semver.
4. Pitfalls: avoid angle-bracket placeholders like `path/<name>.yaml` in doc comments—use `path/{name}.yaml`. Prefer [`crate::Foo`] without a redundant explicit `(crate::Foo)` link target.

No panics required for documentation-only work.
Audit complete: documentation looks consistent with project style. No special verification token is emitted in mock mode.
